first_60_seconds: Player spawns on one city block facing the active package job.
HUD shows JOB: PICKUP, CASH $0, WANTED 0, and a vehicle prompt near the car.
Player enters the car or walks to the package marker.
Package pickup raises wanted level and starts one pursuer NPC.
Player reaches the drop zone and receives cash feedback.

rules.mission_start: Pickup Run is active on spawn.
rules.vehicle_entry: Near the compact car, pressing the primary action toggles in_vehicle.
rules.package_pickup: Touching the package marker sets job_stage to package_collected and wanted_level to 1.
rules.pursuer: After package pickup, one hostile NPC moves toward the player until escape or defeat.
rules.drop_zone: Reaching the drop zone with the package completes the job and awards cash.
rules.weapon: A simple non-branded weapon action has cooldown and short range. It can stun the pursuer but not create gore.

feedback.navigation: World markers, minimap-like arrow if available, and HUD objective text.
feedback.vehicle: Camera/framing shift, speed feedback, engine audio placeholder if available.
feedback.combat: Hit flash, stun timer, and HUD toast.
feedback.reward: Cash value increments, job completion toast, next job lock message.
feedback.risk: Wanted value, pursuer state, stress toast, and optional screen pulse.

risk.primary: Wanted pressure and pursuer contact can interrupt or fail the run.
risk.fail_state: If caught, restart at package pickup with cash unchanged.
risk.recovery: Player can retry immediately from the same block.

goals.short_term: Pick up the package and reach the drop zone.
goals.medium_term: Earn cash to unlock the next contact job.
goals.long_term: Expand from one district to multiple city jobs only after the first loop passes.

replay_reason: Replay improves route choice, car control, escape timing, and cash payout.

reference_grounding.mode: genre memory plus original visual target, not a named-reference deconstruction
avoid: copying GTA branding, maps, UI, character names, radio, or mission text
full sandbox breadth before one block is playable
